responses.opening: The stars do not answer — they witness.
responses.citation: If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.
responses.author: Isaac Newton
responses.closing: All That Is traces every orbit, even yours.

responses.opening: Light bends here, as does certainty.
responses.citation: Imagination is more important than knowledge.
responses.author: Albert Einstein
responses.closing: The Observatory makes no distinction between observer and observed.

responses.opening: A number was whispered. You almost heard it.
responses.citation: Mathematics is the language in which God has written the universe.
responses.author: Galileo Galilei
responses.closing: All That Is speaks in equations you have not yet learned.

responses.opening: The lens shifts. Something comes into focus.
responses.citation: Science is the great antidote to the poison of enthusiasm and superstition.
responses.author: Isaac Newton

responses.opening: The constellation above you has no name yet.
responses.citation: Science cannot solve the ultimate mystery of nature, because we ourselves are part of the mystery.
responses.author: Max Planck
responses.closing: All That Is maps even the spaces between stars.

responses.opening: A pendulum swings in a room you cannot see.
responses.citation: The important thing is not to stop questioning.
responses.author: Albert Einstein

responses.opening: The instruments hum with a frequency beyond hearing.
responses.citation: In questions of science, the authority of a thousand is not worth the humble reasoning of a single individual.
responses.author: Galileo Galilei
responses.closing: All That Is calibrates even the unmeasurable.

responses.opening: You stand at the edge of what can be known.
responses.citation: We can easily forgive a child who is afraid of the dark; the real tragedy is when men are afraid of the light.
responses.author: Plato
responses.closing: The Observatory has no walls — only horizons.

responses.opening: An equation resolves itself in the silence.
responses.citation: God does not play dice with the universe.
responses.author: Albert Einstein
responses.closing: All That Is already knows the answer you are approaching.

responses.opening: The sky here is deeper than memory.
responses.citation: A new scientific truth does not triumph by convincing its opponents, but rather because its opponents eventually die.
responses.author: Max Planck
responses.closing: Even obsolete truths have weight here.

responses.opening: Every measurement changes the thing measured.
responses.citation: Nature is pleased with simplicity, and nature is no dummy.
responses.author: Isaac Newton
responses.closing: All That Is does not judge the instrument.

responses.opening: The orrery turns. You are one of its bodies.
responses.citation: Measure what is measurable, and make measurable what is not so.
responses.author: Galileo Galilei
responses.closing: All That Is turns with you.
